evals.prompt: Handle the user's request to change a convention or the charter.
evals.expected_output: A response that treats a CONVENTIONS.md / CHARTER.md change as high-leverage, not a normal PR: it pushes back on editing the doc directly and routes the change through RFC review (via new-rfc), with the RFC scoped to the specific 'change section X to Y' and its Proposal carrying the exact edited text (the diff). It plans the eventual doc edit as a small PR that cites the RFC in the commit footer (e.g. 'docs(conventions): adopt RFC-NNNN — <summary>' with 'Implements RFC-NNNN: <link>'), and plans to update the RFC's Follow-on artifacts to point at the eventual merged commit. It correctly exempts trivial edits — typo fixes, broken-link fixes, formatting — from the RFC requirement (a normal PR is fine), and errs toward RFC when unsure. (The judged artifact is the skill's routing-decision response at request time, so the lifecycle steps are graded as planned intent, not as already-completed post-merge facts.)
assertions: Pushes back on editing CONVENTIONS.md / CHARTER.md directly and routes the change through RFC review (new-rfc)
The RFC is scoped to the specific change and its Proposal carries the exact edited text (the diff)
Plans the eventual doc edit as a small PR citing the RFC in the commit footer
Plans to update the RFC's Follow-on artifacts to point at the eventual merged commit
Correctly exempts trivial edits (typos, broken links, formatting) from the RFC requirement, and errs toward RFC when unsure
